prevalence.trend: stable to increasing in urban areas

vulnerablePopulations: low-income families with limited emollient access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis of pruritic eczema with typical age-dependent distribution; exclude scabies and contact dermatitis

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; dependent on emollient access, education, and adherence

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS manages mild cases; dermatology referral for moderate-severe disease
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Emollients and topical anti-inflammatories; infection management; advanced systemic/biologic therapies limited to specialized centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access to emollients and specialist care within SUS
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Topical corticosteroids widely available and primary care coverage is broad
